short_description.value: RingMoE is a 14.7-billion-parameter multi-modal remote sensing foundation model, pre-trained on 400 million images from nine satellites across four modalities (Optical, Multi-spectral, SAR-L1, SAR-L2). It uses a hierarchical Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with modal-specialized, collaborative, and shared experts, incorporates physics-informed self-supervised learning, and supports dynamic expert pruning for efficient deployment. RingMoE achieves SOTA results on 23 out of 25 public benchmarks across six key remote sensing tasks.

pretext_training_type.value: Masked image modeling (MIM) with physics-informed self-supervised learning

masking_strategy.value: Random masking of image patches; physics-informed masking for SAR-L1

pretraining.value: Self-supervised masked image reconstruction with modal-specific decoders and hierarchical Mixture-of-Experts encoder

value.value: Replace FFN with hierarchical Mixture-of-Experts (RMoE) layer
Global (six continents, 57 countries, e.g., United States, China, Japan)

cloud_cover.value: Tiles with cloud cover automatically detected and removed
